tldr: Headlines announce Xi's Pyongyang visit overwhelmingly as a bilateral diplomatic event — pledges, blueprints, and new-era consensus — while DPRK-watcher and South Korean outlets lead instead with nuclear arsenal expansion and the summit's silence on denuclearization, and Western outlets cast China and North Korea as US adversaries restoring a strategic alliance.

evidence.quote: Xi, Kim pledge to carry forward traditional China-DPRK
Xi, Kim pledge to advance China-Korea ties in latest talks
Kim, Xi unveil 'far-reaching blueprint' to deepen
Xi and Kim Agree to Strengthen 'Strategic Relationship'

frames.sub_frame: DPRK nuclear arms vs. denuclearization silence

evidence.quote: Kim Jong Un unveils new nuclear enrichment plant
Kim, Xi vow stronger North Korea-China ties, silent
denuclearization remains goal despite absence from Xi-Kim

frames.sub_frame: China reclaiming DPRK from Russian orbit

evidence.quote: Xi Seeks to Draw North Korea Back Into China's Orbit
US adversaries China, North Korea strengthening ties

frames.sub_frame: Xi must press Kim on denuclearization

evidence.quote: To denuclearize North Korea, Xi must make Kim an offer

bottom_line: The headline layer presents the Xi-Kim summit as a triumph of restored bilateral bonds, but rival outlets in the same cycle counter with nuclear arsenal reveals and denuclearization-silence leads — the advertised diplomatic warmth and the security subtext run in direct opposition across the corpus.
